title: Safe Language AST Schema
description: JSON schema mapping BNF productions from spec/08-syntax-summary.md to AST node types. FINAL -- all draft notes resolved.

meta.note: Each node definition maps a BNF production to a PascalCase AST node type. Fields specify name, type, and whether the field is optional. source_section references the spec section defining the production. clause_ids (where present) reference normative clauses from clauses/clauses.yaml that the node directly implements.

type_conventions.Identifier: String -- a lowercase Safe identifier (lowercase letter followed by lowercase letters, digits, underscores)
type_conventions.NodeRef<T>: Non-null reference to an AST node of type T
type_conventions.Option<T>: Nullable reference -- field may be absent
type_conventions.List<T>: Ordered sequence of zero or more AST nodes of type T
type_conventions.NonEmptyList<T>: Ordered sequence of one or more AST nodes of type T
type_conventions.Token: Lexical token preserved for source fidelity
type_conventions.Literal: A literal value (numeric, string, or character)
type_conventions.Span: Source location span (file, start_line, start_col, end_line, end_col)

meta.production_coverage_note: The BNF grammar in spec/08-syntax-summary.md defines approximately 148 named productions. Many are abstract alternation productions (e.g., type_definition, constraint, simple_statement, compound_statement), alias productions (e.g., subtype_mark ::= name, condition ::= expression, choice_expression ::= simple_expression), or lexical-level productions (e.g., identifier, digit, letter). These do not require distinct AST node types. Instead, they are represented by discriminant enumerations (e.g., TypeDefinitionKind, ConstraintKind, StatementKind) or are structurally identical to their referent. The concrete AST nodes below cover all syntactically distinct constructs.
meta.semantic_annotations_note: Certain semantic properties -- ownership state, wide-arithmetic lifting, resolved types, resolved SelectedComponent kinds -- are computed during semantic analysis and are not set by the parser. Rather than adding separate annotation layers, these are represented as optional fields on the relevant AST nodes (marked 'populated during semantic analysis'). This keeps the schema self-contained.

fields.name: kind
fields.note: Discriminant: one of BasicDeclaration, TaskDeclaration, ChannelDeclaration, UseTypeClause, RepresentationItem, Pragma

fields.name: item
fields.type: NodeRef<BasicDeclaration | TaskDeclaration | ChannelDeclaration | UseTypeClause | RepresentationItem | Pragma>

fields.name: kind
fields.note: SubtypeOnly (new T) or Annotated (new (Expr as T))

fields.name: resolved_kind
fields.note: Populated during semantic analysis: RecordField, Attribute, PackageMember, ImplicitDereference. The parser creates SelectedComponent for all X.Y forms; semantic analysis classifies each case per the resolution rule in spec/02-restrictions.md section 2.4.1.

fields.name: formal_name
fields.note: Named association selector; absent for positional

fields.name: logical_operator
fields.note: And, AndThen, Or, OrElse, Xor; absent for single-relation expression. Ada's rule that mixed logical operators require parentheses is enforced by the parser.

fields.name: resolved_type
fields.note: Populated during semantic analysis: the resolved type of this expression

fields.name: wide_arithmetic
fields.note: Populated during semantic analysis: true if this expression or any subexpression requires wide-integer lifting per D27 Rule 1

fields.name: kind
fields.note: NumericLiteral, StringLiteral, CharacterLiteral, Null, Name, Allocator, Aggregate, ParenExpr, AnnotatedExpr, ConditionalExpr, DeclareExpr

fields.name: value
fields.type: NodeRef<Literal | Name | Allocator | Aggregate | Expression | AnnotatedExpression | ConditionalExpression | DeclareExpression>

nodes.note: Aggregate disambiguation (record vs. array vs. delta) may not be fully resolvable at parse time. The parser may produce a generic Aggregate node; semantic analysis refines the variant. This schema presents the post-resolution forms.

fields.name: items
fields.note: May contain both statements and interleaved declarations; empty suites are permitted after PR11.6.2 source `null` removal.

fields.name: kind
fields.note: Discriminant for the actual statement node

fields.name: ownership_action
fields.note: Populated during semantic analysis: Move (source nulled), None, or Dealloc (target freed before assign)

fields.name: resolved_value
fields.note: Populated during semantic analysis: the parsed numeric value as a decimal string for use by the emitter

nodes.note: Raw text is stored for source fidelity. The semantic analysis phase parses and evaluates the literal value into resolved_value for use during emission and range analysis.

enumerations.name: SelectedComponentKind
enumerations.note: Populated during semantic analysis; not set by parser. The parser creates SelectedComponent for all X.Y forms. Semantic analysis classifies each based on the type/kind of the prefix per the resolution rule in spec/02-restrictions.md section 2.4.1.

enumerations.name: OwnershipAction
enumerations.note: Populated during semantic analysis on AssignmentStatement nodes for access type assignments. 'Move' indicates the source is nulled after assignment per spec/02-restrictions.md section 2.3.2.

enumerations.name: AggregateKind
enumerations.note: Used by the parser when aggregate form is ambiguous. Semantic analysis resolves to the specific aggregate node type.

abstract_productions_coverage.note: The following BNF productions are abstract alternation productions, alias productions, or lexical productions that do not require distinct AST node types. They are covered by discriminant enumerations, structural identity with their referent, or the lexer.

alias_productions.note: Structurally identical (Safe has no exception handlers)
Represented as a list field on containing nodes
Represented as a list field on containing nodes

name_resolution_note: Name resolution ambiguity: IndexedComponent, Slice, TypeConversion, and FunctionCall all share the syntactic form name(args). The parser produces a generic CallOrIndex node during parsing. Semantic analysis reclassifies it into the correct concrete form. This schema presents the post-resolution forms. The parser implementation may use an intermediate node type not listed here.
